app.title: RotorTcpBridge
about.title: About RotorTcpBridge
about.lbl_apache_intro: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. You may use this application only in compliance with the License. A copy of the License is bundled with the installation and is also available online.
about.lbl_apache_warranty: The software is distributed "AS IS", without warranties or conditions of any kind, express or implied, including – but not limited to – warranties of merchantability or fitness for a particular purpose. See the license text for details.
about.lbl_com0com_intro: This application optionally uses com0com (null-modem emulator) to create paired virtual serial ports on Windows. com0com is installed separately as a standalone Windows kernel driver and does not run inside this application's process – there is no code linkage.
about.lbl_com0com_license: com0com is released under the GNU General Public License, version 2. All rights and the complete source code remain with the com0com authors (Vyacheslav Frolov and others). We gratefully acknowledge their work and are happy to rely on this free software.
warn_err.title: Warnings and errors
main.ref_failed_msg:
Homing {axis} could not be started.
The rotor did not respond with ACK_SETREF.

Please press «Homing» again.
settings.title: RotorTcpBridge - Settings
settings.map_aswatch_only_asnearest_list: Show only top connections on the map
When checked, the map only draws stations that also appear in the “Next connections” (ASNEAREST) list. Other user markers are not shown (no extra map work for them).
settings.map_aswatch_cluster_tooltip: On: zoomed out only cluster circles with counts; as soon as zooming in shows individual markers, full user icons appear. Off: compact labels at low zoom, zoom in further for full markers.
rig_bridge.new_name_prompt: Name of the new rig profile:
rig_bridge.rename_prompt: New name for the rig profile:
rig_bridge.cannot_delete_last: At least one rig profile must remain.
rig.enabled_tooltip: Turns the entire Rig-Bridge on or off. Only when active the CAT connection to the radio is opened and the TCP protocols (Flrig/Hamlib) can serve clients.
rig.btn_test_tooltip:
Sends set-frequency to 144.300 MHz (CAT per brand, e.g. Yaesu FA…;) and logs RX.
With an active “Connect” session: same COM port, briefly serialized against the worker.
Without a connection: the COM port is opened separately for a moment. A bare digit in the port is resolved to COMn (Windows).
rig.radio_brand_tooltip: Radio manufacturer. Defines which models are available below and which CAT protocol is used.
rig.radio_model_tooltip: Specific radio model. Determines the exact CAT variant and the associated Hamlib rig ID.
rig.hamlib_model_id_tooltip: Internal Hamlib identifier (rigctl number) of the selected model. Set automatically, shown for information only.
rig.auto_connect: Connect to radio at program start
rig.auto_connect_tooltip: If Rig-Bridge is active: open the COM port when the application starts. Flrig and Hamlib (rigctld) then start automatically if their autostart is enabled.
rig.auto_reconnect_tooltip: Preference for restoring the COM session in future (configuration); the Rig-Bridge uses it once the corresponding logic is wired up.
rig.com_port_tooltip:
Serial COM port of the radio (CAT).
After unplugging/replugging USB or changing ports: refresh the list with “↻”.
Bare digits are resolved to COMn on Windows.
In the opened list: hover over an entry to see the driver/device description.
rig.com_saved_not_available: Saved in configuration, currently not in the Windows port list.
rig.baud_tooltip: UART baud rate of the CAT interface per radio manual (typically 38400, 9600 or 115200). Must match the device, otherwise CAT communication is unreliable.
rig.serial_frame: Serial frame (fixed): 8 data bits · 1 stop bit · no parity (8N1)
rig.serial_frame_tooltip:
Fixed and not configurable: 8 data bits, 1 stop bit, no parity (8N1).
Matches the typical CAT UART used by most manufacturers.
rig.timeout_tooltip:
Maximum wait per serial CAT operation in seconds (read/write on the COM port).
Too small: timeouts or aborted commands on slow devices or long cables.
Too large: longer blocking if the device does not respond.
rig.poll_tooltip:
Interval in milliseconds between periodic CAT queries (e.g. frequency) when clients or the display need current values from the radio.
Smaller = faster updates, more load on COM and device; larger = less traffic.
rig.cat_drain_tooltip: After each CAT write (FA/TX/MD): wait at most this long for an echo or `;` (milliseconds). Too large = slow WSJT-X rounds; too small = echo may be missed on some devices.
rig.setfreq_gap_tooltip: Short pause after each serial SETFREQ (FA…;) in milliseconds. Helps if the radio replies with ``?;`` or skips steps when tuning quickly. 0 = no additional pause.
rig.btn_connect_tooltip:
Opens the serial CAT session on the selected COM port at the configured baud rate.
Check brand/model first; afterwards Flrig/Hamlib can share the same session.
rig.btn_disconnect_tooltip:
Closes the CAT connection to the radio.
Please stop running TCP services (Flrig/Hamlib) separately with “Stop” if active.
rig.flrig_active_tooltip: Enables the Flrig-compatible XML-RPC/TCP server. Other programs (e.g. fldigi, WSJT-X in Flrig mode) can then control the radio through this bridge.
rig.flrig_host_tooltip: Enter the local IP on which Flrig runs, or 127.0.0.1.
rig.flrig_port_tooltip: Enter the port the application should connect to Flrig on. Default is 12345.
rig.flrig_autostart_tooltip: Automatically start the Flrig server as soon as the Rig-Bridge is running and the CAT connection to the radio is up.
rig.flrig_log_tcp_tooltip: Logs connections, incoming HTTP requests, XML-RPC methods/parameters, and responses to the application and Rig diagnostics log. Leave on while debugging WSJT-X/fldigi in Flrig mode; turn off if too noisy.
rig.flrig_btn_start_tooltip: Start the Flrig server now without restarting the application. Requires an active CAT connection.
rig.flrig_btn_stop_tooltip: Stop the Flrig server immediately. Connected clients will be disconnected.
rig.hamlib_active_tooltip: Enables the Hamlib NET rigctl server (compatible with rigctld). External programs such as WSJT-X, JTDX, MSHV or Gpredict can connect to the ports configured below.
rig.hamlib_host_tooltip: Enter the local IP on which the service should run, or 127.0.0.1.
rig.hamlib_port_tooltip: Enter the port the application should connect to. Default is 4532. Each new connection needs its own port.
rig.hamlib_name_tooltip: Only helps identify which software is attached here.
rig.hamlib_ports_header: Ports / program (one listener per line, same host above):
rig.hamlib_btn_add_row_tooltip: Adds another listener row so an additional program can be connected in parallel on its own port.
rig.hamlib_autostart_tooltip: Automatically start the Hamlib NET server as soon as the Rig-Bridge is running and the CAT connection to the radio is up.
rig.hamlib_debug_tooltip: Writes additional diagnostic information (timing intervals and long replies) to the log. Enable only for troubleshooting – leave off during normal operation.
rig.hamlib_log_tcp_tooltip: Log TCP commands and replies from Hamlib NET listeners (independent of serial CAT logging).
rig.hamlib_btn_start_tooltip: Start the Hamlib NET rigctl server now without restarting the application. Requires an active CAT connection.
rig.hamlib_btn_stop_tooltip: Stop the Hamlib NET rigctl server immediately. All connected clients (WSJT-X, etc.) will be disconnected.
settings.chk_udp_ucxlog_tooltip: Listens on the selected IP and port (default 127.0.0.1:12040). UcxLog must use the same IP/port. When “Set Rotor” is used, UcxLog sends UDP XML with azimuth to drive the rotor.
settings.ucxlog_udp_listen_tooltip: IPv4 to listen on (0.0.0.0 = all interfaces).
settings.aswatch_udp_listen_tooltip: IPv4 to listen on (0.0.0.0 = all interfaces).
settings.udp_pst_listen_tooltip: IPv4 to listen on. Windows only delivers packets that were sent exactly to this address: 127.0.0.1 = only PST programs on this machine (recommended); this PC's LAN IP = packets from the network, but only those addressed to this IP; 0.0.0.0 = all interfaces (will also pick up foreign rotor setups in the LAN).
settings.chk_aswatch_udp_tooltip: When KST4Contest is running together with an AirScout instance, the positions of partner stations from the KST chat can be shown on the map. Only enable this option in that combination.
settings.chk_aswatch_aircraft_tooltip: When off: ASNEAREST is not processed (no aircraft icons, no lines to the partner station, no asnearest.jsonl logging). ASWATCHLIST/ASSETPATH stay active.
settings.asnearest_min_score_tooltip: Final score = AirScout base × mid-path factor (maximum at half the path QTH↔partner). Only entries with score ≥ this value appear in the map panel and as aircraft/lines.
settings.asnearest_geom_min_tooltip: How “central” the reflection point is on the radio path between your QTH and the partner station. AirScout gives a distance along that line; from it we compute t (0 = at you, 1 = at the partner) and g = 4×t×(1−t). g peaks at the path midpoint and drops toward the ends (scatter right over QTH or over the target is usually implausible). The 0–100 value is g as a percent: 0 = no extra threshold (other ASNEAREST limits still apply). Higher = stricter: show the reflection line only if g is at least this value (e.g. 20 → g ≥ 0.20). App default: 20.
settings.asnearest_list_max_minutes_tool: The ASNEAREST table on the map only lists rows whose shown remaining time (minutes) is at most this value. 0 = no limit (all that meet the minimum score). Example: 5 shows only connections with at most 5 minutes left.
settings.asnearest_list_max_rows_tooltip: Maximum number of rows in the ASNEAREST table on the map (1–500).
settings.chk_udp_pst_tooltip: Emulates the UDP protocol of PstRotatorAz. Receives control commands on the specified port. Position reports (AZ:/TGA:) go to “Target IP”:port+1.
settings.udp_pst_port_tooltip: Listen port (default: 12000). Position reports are sent to target IP:port+1.
settings.udp_pst_send_host_tooltip: IPv4 for outgoing AZ:/TGA: datagrams (port remains port+1). Empty / new: automatic subnet broadcast (e.g. 192.168.1.255). 127.0.0.1 = this PC only. Another host: its IP, e.g. 192.168.1.50. 255.255.255.255 = subnet-wide broadcast.
settings.cal_start_not_connected: No connection to the hardware (serial/TCP). Please connect first.
settings.cal_start_axis_offline_az: The azimuth axis is not reachable (no bus response). Check the connection.
settings.cal_start_axis_offline_el: The elevation axis is not reachable (no bus response). Check the connection.
settings.cal_start_need_ref: The rotor is not homed yet. Please run a reference move (REFF) first.
settings.cal_description:
Calibration measures motor current over one full rotation and stores it
as a reference for later comparisons (e.g. load profile).
Run calibration again after changes to the antenna or mechanics.
settings.stats_help_mid:
The fields under “Azimuth” and “Elevation” apply separately and control how load values (mV, 0–65535) are colored on the compass ring and in the statistics views.

• Custom color scale: When off, each view auto-scales from minimum to maximum (blue to red). When on, the fixed thresholds and normal band are used.

• Lower threshold (blue), normal min/max (green), upper threshold (red): With a custom scale the order must be: blue ≤ normal min ≤ normal max ≤ red.

• Apply values from calibration: Takes min/max from the completed current calibration (CAL), sets normal min/max and thresholds with a ±50 mV margin, and enables the custom scale.
settings.shortcuts_enabled_tooltip: Works even when no application window is in the foreground. Shortcuts may conflict with other software.
settings.shortcuts_win_only: Global shortcuts are only supported on Windows.
settings.shortcuts_modifiers_hint: Pick two modifiers (or “—” plus one key for a single modifier). Applies to all keys listed below.
settings.shortcuts_modifier_tooltip: Alt, Ctrl, Shift, or Windows; “—” means no modifier in that slot.
settings.controller_hw_enable_tooltip: Off: no separate controller on the bus — fields below are local (config) only; no GET/SET commands are sent to the controller.
settings.controller_broadcast_disabled: The hardware controller is disabled. Enable “Use hardware controller” to send SETCONIDF.
settings.controller_id_tooltip: 0–245. Must not equal this application’s RS485 master ID (see Connection).
settings.controller_wind_anemo_tooltip: On the controller: enable anemometer (SETCONANO 1) or disable (SETCONANO 0). Value is sent on save via RS485; opening the tab reads GETCONANO.
settings.antenna_realign_on_switch_toolt: On: After antenna switch (SETPOSDG), same bearing as before. Off: display offsets only on the compass. Works without hardware controller. With hardware controller: on save, SETCONCHA is sent.
settings.controller_antenna_realign_tool: On: after switch SETPOSDG, same bearing. Off: display offset only. On save also SETCONCHA on the controller hardware.
settings.controller_display_brightness_t: Brightness of the controller LED ring, 0–100% (SETCONLEDP / GETCONLEDP). Sent to the controller when you release the slider.
settings.controller_status_write_fail: At least one controller value could not be written.
settings.controller_err_same_as_master: The controller ID must not match this bridge’s RS485 master ID (currently {mid}). Please choose another value.
settings.controller_setconidf_tooltip: Sends SETCONIDF to broadcast address 255 with the controller ID entered in the field (0–245). Important: Only one controller may be on the RS485 bus. If several controllers are connected, they will all receive the same new address.
settings.controller_setconidf_offline: No hardware connection – cannot send SETCONIDF.
settings.controller_led_tooltip: Green: all controller values were read from the bus. Red: no connection, read not possible, or not all responses were valid.
settings.controller_wait_tooltip: Controller query in progress — shown until the status LED turns green or red.
settings.compass_om_radar_sectors_toolti: Number of sectors around the compass (10–100). Higher = finer heatmap, lower = coarser. Default 60 (6° each).
settings.compass_dwell_sectors_tooltip: Number of sectors for the dwell ring (10–100), independent of OM radar.
settings.compass_dwell_minutes_tooltip: Linear colour scale: 0 s = blue, this cumulative dwell time in a sector = red. Only standstill counts (not while moving). Session only until restart.
settings.stats_cal_waiting: No CAL data (run calibration or wait …).
settings.stats_cal_no_data: No CAL bins available. Finish calibration first (CAL state complete).
settings.stats_heatmap_thr_blue_tooltip: Below this: significantly lower load (blue). Order: blue ≤ normal min ≤ normal max ≤ red.
settings.stats_heatmap_norm_tooltip: Between normal min and max the load is considered normal (green band).
settings.stats_apply_from_cal_tooltip: Takes min/max from the last CAL run and sets thresholds with ±50 mV margin.
settings.stats_heatmap_invalid: Heatmap: please set blue ≤ normal min ≤ normal max ≤ red.
settings.stats_heatmap_info: Without a custom scale, blue is the minimum and red the maximum value in each view (long-term and current use the same mapping). With a custom scale: green in the normal band; only below the blue or above the red threshold does the color shift clearly toward blue or red – for the compass ring and the statistics window (CAL, long-term, and current).
settings.antenna_names_led_tooltip: Green: all three display names (GETCONANTNAME1–3) were last read from the bus successfully. Red: offline, query skipped, or invalid response.
settings.msgbox_az_error:
The AZ offset values could not be saved in the rotor.
Please check the connection and try again.
settings.location_locator_tooltip: Maidenhead grid, e.g. JO31jg. If latitude/longitude are still the defaults, the map uses the field center. The button fills lat/lon offline from the cell center.
settings.btn_locator_apply_coords_toolti: Set latitude and longitude to the center of the entered locator (local math only, no internet).
settings.locator_empty: Please enter a Maidenhead locator (e.g. JO31jg).
settings.locator_invalid: Invalid or too short locator. Allowed: 2–10 characters (AA, AA00, …).
settings.antenna_height_tooltip: Height of the antenna above ground (mast height). Added to terrain elevation in the elevation profile.
settings.tooltip_master_id: RS485 bus: address of this PC/bridge as master (0–255). Must match the rotor firmware configuration.
settings.tooltip_slave_az: RS485 slave ID of the azimuth axis (1–254). As set in the rotor firmware.
settings.tooltip_slave_el: RS485 slave ID of the elevation axis (1–254). As set in the rotor firmware.
settings.chk_pst_enabled_tooltip: Starts the built-in PST server (TCP) so PST Rotator can control the axes. In PST Rotator set the rotor type to “SPID BIG-RAS” – the protocol must match your rotor.
settings.pst_listen_host_tooltip: IP address the PST server listens on (often 0.0.0.0 for all interfaces or 127.0.0.1 for local only).
settings.pst_port_az_tooltip: TCP port for PST control of the azimuth axis (often 4001).
settings.pst_port_el_tooltip: TCP port for PST control of the elevation axis (often 4002).
settings.hw_mode_tooltip: tcp: Connection via TCP/IP to the RS485 bus – you need a TCP-to-RS485 converter, e.g. EBYTE NE2-D11/P (Ethernet) or for Wi-Fi e.g. PUSR USR-DR164. com: Direct serial link – e.g. USB–RS485 adapter or the RotorTcpBridge hardware controller.
settings.hw_ip_tooltip: IP address of the TCP–RS485 converter or device that exposes the RS485 bus.
settings.hw_port_tooltip: TCP port of the converter (as set in the device/web UI). Typical factory defaults: EBYTE NE2-D11: 8886, PUSR USR-DR164: 8899.
settings.hw_com_tooltip: COM port of the USB–RS485 adapter or hardware controller (serial).
settings.btn_com_refresh_tooltip: Refresh the COM port list from the OS (after plugging in an adapter).
settings.baudrate_tooltip: Serial baud rate on the RS485 bus (defined by rotor/converter; shown for info).
settings.chk_enable_az_tooltip: Enable the azimuth axis: polling and commands over RS485/PST.
settings.chk_enable_el_tooltip: Enable the elevation axis: polling and commands over RS485/PST. At least one axis (AZ or EL) must stay enabled.
settings.chk_dark_mode_tooltip: Always use the dark theme for the UI (independent of Windows).
settings.wind_dir_display_tooltip: How wind direction is shown on the map: meteorological “from” or “to”.
settings.location_lat_tooltip: Antenna site latitude (map, compass, elevation profile).
settings.location_lon_tooltip: Antenna site longitude (map, compass, elevation profile).
settings.tooltip_antenna_name: Display name for this antenna (compass and map). Characters # : $ are not allowed in the protocol and cannot be typed. Max. 9 characters (hardware controller).
settings.tooltip_antenna_offset: Azimuth offset for this antenna in degrees (offset in the rotor).
settings.tooltip_antenna_angle: Beam opening angle in degrees (cone on the map).
settings.tooltip_antenna_range: Beam range on the map in km (display only).
cmd.title: Rotor Configuration
cmd.tooltip_group_build: Select a command, enter parameters, send as RS485 frame, or use backup / restore.
cmd.tooltip_dst: Destination on the RS485 bus (slave ID of the AZ or EL axis).
cmd.tooltip_cmd: Pick a command from the list or type the name. Hover list entries for catalog tooltips.
cmd.tooltip_params: Parameters per protocol (e.g. angles, comma-separated). Enter in this field sends the command.
cmd.tooltip_frame: Preview of the RS485 text frame to be sent (read-only).
cmd.tooltip_cmd_info: Short description and notes for the selected command.
cmd.tooltip_btn_send: Send the current frame to the rotor.
cmd.tooltip_btn_backup: Save supported rotor parameters to an XML file.
cmd.tooltip_btn_restore: Apply parameters from a backup XML file to the rotor.
cmd.hint_default: Tip: Enter in PARAMS field sends the command; Enter in parameters sends Set.
cmd.hint_enter: Tip: Enter sends the current command.
cmd.msgbox_restore_done: {total} SET commands were sent to the hardware.
cmd.tooltip_current_ma_note: Input in mA (converted to mV internally, 1 A = 130 mV).
cmd.tooltip_timeout_s_note: Input in seconds (converted to/from ms internally).
cmd.tooltip_setpostimeout_s: Timeout for position move until target is reached.
cmd.tooltip_sethometimeout_s: Timeout for homing procedure until reference point.
cmd.tooltip_setiwarn_ma: Set current warning threshold. Warning only, no stop.
cmd.tooltip_setimax_ma: Set current shutdown threshold. Leads to stop + error.
log.title: RotorTcpBridge - Log
weather.title: Weather
stats.title: Statistics
popup.error_footer_restart: Inspect the rotor and mechanics on site (limit switches, wiring, blockage). While an error is shown, homing (SETREF) often cannot clear the fault reliably. Close this message and restart the application so the display and bus status match again.
popup.error_footer_not_homed: Run homing first (Homing button). If the error persists, check the rotor mechanics on site and restart the application so the display and bus status match again.
popup.error_footer_deadman: Check bridge/master: keepalive and deadman interval. While an error is shown, homing often cannot clear the fault reliably. Close this message and restart the application so the display and bus status match again.
popup.err.d1.name: ERR_HOME_TIMEOUT
popup.err.d1.check: Check limit switches, count parameters, and HOMETIMEOUT; inspect the rotor and mechanics on site (cables, blockage, free movement).
popup.err.d2.name: ERR_MOVE_TIMEOUT
popup.err.d2.check: Check mechanics and load, adjust MOVETIMEOUT; inspect the rotor on site for blockage and end stops.
popup.err.d3.name: ERR_NOT_HOMED
popup.err.d3.cause: A position move was requested but homing has not been completed.
popup.err.d3.check: Start homing first (Homing button). If that does not help: check reference/limit switches and mechanics on the rotor.
popup.err.d13.name: ERR_JAM_DETECTED
popup.err.d13.cause: Jam: too little movement within the JAM window.
popup.err.d13.check: Check mechanics and stiction, review JAM parameters; inspect the rotor on site for jamming.
popup.err.d14.name: ERR_DEADMAN_TIMEOUT
popup.err.d14.cause: Deadman timeout: no valid RS485 request within the time window.
popup.err.d14.check: Check master and bridge: adjust keepalive/deadman interval, wiring, and supply.
popup.err.l10.name: SE_TIMEOUT
popup.err.l10.cause: Firmware reports error code 10 (GETERR/ACK_ERR): deadman/keepalive — not the same as “no reply” from this app; traffic may still appear in the log.
popup.err.l10.check: Bridge/master: send enough RS485 traffic during movement (keepalive/deadman interval); check cables and deadman parameters on the rotor.
popup.err.l11.name: SE_ENDSTOP
popup.err.l11.cause: A limit switch blocks the requested direction of travel.
popup.err.l11.check: Check direction, limit switches, and offsets; inspect rotor mechanics on site.
popup.err.l15.name: SE_IS_HARD
popup.err.l15.cause: Current hard limit: motor current too high for longer than hold time.
popup.err.l15.check: Check mechanics, IMAX, grace, and hold; inspect the rotor and wiring on site.
popup.err.l16.name: SE_STALL
popup.err.l16.cause: Stall: encoder does not move despite PWM.
popup.err.l16.check: Check mechanics, stiction, MINPWM/KICK/STALLTIMEOUT; inspect the rotor on site for blockage.
popup.err.l18.name: SE_POS_TIMEOUT
popup.err.l18.cause: Position move timeout: target not reached within posTimeoutMs.
popup.err.l18.check: Check SETPOSTIMEOUT, ramps, and PWM; mechanics blocked or ramp too soft.
popup.err.m12.name: ERR_OVERCURRENT / SE_NSTOP_CMD (legacy)
popup.err.m12.body:
Two firmware variants use code 0012 for different faults:

• Documentation (ERR_OVERCURRENT): overcurrent, IMAX with OCHOLD/OCIGN.
• Legacy (SE_NSTOP_CMD): emergency stop via RS485.

Inspect the rotor and mechanics on site (limit switches, cables, blockage), power supply, and the RS485 side (master/bridge). While an error is shown, homing (SETREF) often cannot clear the fault reliably. Close this message and restart the application so the display and bus status match again.
popup.err.m17.name: ERR_MOTOR_BEGIN_FAILED / SE_HOME_FAIL (legacy)
popup.err.m17.body:
Two firmware variants use code 0017 for different faults:

• Documentation (ERR_MOTOR_BEGIN_FAILED): PWM/driver init failed (e.g. after SETPWMF/SETINVERT).
• Legacy (SE_HOME_FAIL): homing failed (timeout or limit switch).

Inspect the rotor and mechanics on site, PWM/driver settings, HOMETIMEOUT, and limit switches. While an error is shown, homing (SETREF) often cannot clear the fault reliably. Close this message and restart the application so the display and bus status match again.
popup.err.unknown.name: SE_UNKNOWN
popup.err.unknown.check: Check rotor log, firmware revision, and documentation.
compass.title: Compass
compass.locator_tooltip: Maidenhead grid (2–10 characters). The target is the center of the cell; longer locators are more precise. Bearing from your station in Settings (same as the map) uses the WGS-84 ellipsoid (geodesic), not a simple sphere.
compass.locator_invalid_body: Enter a valid Maidenhead locator (2–10 characters, pairs of letters/digits).
compass.heatmap_om_radar_tooltip: Distribution of AirScout/KST partner stations by geographic bearing. Only stations within the range in km entered for the selected antenna (same as on the map); more distant ones are not counted. Each counted station is spread uniformly across the opening angle (GETANGLE1–3, otherwise settings).
compass.heatmap_az_rings_tooltip: Up to two rings (inner→outer: current map, OM radar, dwell time).
compass.btn_reset_dwell_tooltip: Clear cumulative dwell times in the dwell ring (outer “Dwell time” ring; session only until restart). Does not reset OM radar.
map.title: Antenna Map
map.tooltip_antenna: Which antenna / azimuth beam is shown on the map.
map.tooltip_fav_name: Short name for the favorite (current map target).
map.tooltip_fav_save: Save the current map target under this name.
map.tooltip_fav_delete: Remove the selected favorite from the list.
map.tooltip_elevation: Terrain profile and propagation between home and map target.
map.tooltip_offline: Use locally cached map tiles (no internet).
map.tooltip_locator: Show Maidenhead locator grid on the map.
map.tooltip_ref_az: Start azimuth homing (set current position as reference).
elevation.title: Elevation Profile  –  {home}  →  {target}
elevation.freq_tooltip: Transmit frequency for propagation analysis. VHF/UHF (≥ 30 MHz): Knife-edge diffraction per ITU-R P.526. HF (< 30 MHz): ground wave, NVIS, E- and F2-layer, skip zone and MUF estimate. Common bands: 7 MHz (40 m), 14 MHz (20 m), 144 MHz (2 m), 432 MHz (70 cm)
elevation.chk_live_tooltip: Propagation forecast with live data from NOAA SWPC (F10.7 Solar Flux). When checked: foF2/foE calculated from current F10.7.
elevation.obstacle_label: O b s t a c l e  h={h} m
elevation.tooltip_obstacle: O b s t a c l e at {dist} km (+{h} m above path LOS)
elevation.tooltip_no_obstacle: No o b s t a c l e on this path segment
elevation.offline_no_profile:
Elevation profile not available (no elevation data).
Band recommendation and propagation modes use default values (daily mean).
elevation.tooltip_card_home: Antenna height above mean sea level. Terrain at your site plus mast height (if set).
elevation.tooltip_card_target: Terrain height at the target (distant station) above sea level — not their antenna.
elevation.tooltip_card_max_terrain: Highest terrain point along the path between antenna and target.
elevation.tooltip_card_distance: Great-circle distance between antenna location and target.
elevation.tooltip_card_los: Whether the straight line of sight is blocked by terrain along the path.
elevation.tooltip_diff_j: Extra loss from diffraction at the sharpest obstacle (decibels). Higher = more attenuation.
elevation.tooltip_diff_nu: Diffraction parameter ν at the obstacle (ITU-R P.526). Larger ν: obstacle sits higher above the path — usually more loss (see J(ν)).
elevation.tooltip_diff_power: Remaining signal vs. an unobstructed path (100 % = no knife-edge loss).
elevation.tooltip_diff_rating: Qualitative diffraction grade per ITU-R P.526 (single obstacle).
elevation.tooltip_opt_band: Suggested amateur band and frequency for this distance and propagation.
elevation.tooltip_offline_home_coords: Antenna site: latitude and longitude (decimal degrees).
elevation.tooltip_offline_target_coords: Target point: latitude and longitude (decimal degrees).
elevation.tooltip_skip_zone_card: No practical ionospheric path for this distance and frequency.
elevation.tooltip_mode_groundwave: Typical ground-wave range on this frequency (HF).
elevation.tooltip_mode_nvis: NVIS: near-vertical reflection via F2 — typically 0–500 km.
elevation.tooltip_mode_e_layer: E-layer: skip distances and number of hops.
elevation.tooltip_mode_f2_layer: F2 layer: MUF is the highest frequency still reflected on this hop.
elevation.tooltip_mode_generic: Ionospheric propagation: numbers describe range and usability.
catalog.help.STOP: Soft stop (normal braking). Ends a position move. Errors are NOT cleared.
catalog.help.NSTOP: Emergency stop via RS485. Immediately sets error SE_NSTOP_CMD and stops.
catalog.help.GETREF: Query whether reference (homing) is available. Response: 0 = not homed, 1 = homed.
catalog.help.GETPOSDG: Current position in degrees (0.01°). Return value is always the offset-corrected position.
catalog.help.GETIS: Current measurement value (mV) after offset subtraction. Selected from IS1/IS2 based on direction of movement.
catalog.help.GETTEMPA: Ambient temperature in °C. DS18B20, 2 decimal places.
catalog.help.GETTEMPM: Motor temperature in °C. If motor sensor disabled: always 0.
catalog.help.SETANEMOOF: Set wind meter offset (km/h). Recommended: -5 to +5. Helpful if sensor does not show 0 at calm wind.
catalog.help.GETANEMOOF: Read wind speed offset (km/h) (1 decimal place, e.g. -1.5). Value from NVS/Preferences key ano.
catalog.help.SETWINDENABLE: Enable (1) / disable (0) wind evaluation. A wind sensor must be connected to the rotor.
catalog.help.GETWINDENABLE: Read wind evaluation active status. 0 = off, 1 = on.
catalog.help.GETANEMO: Wind speed from anemometer in km/h. 0..200 km/h, 1 decimal place, including offset.
catalog.help.GETBEAUFORT: Wind strength as Beaufort value (0–12). 0 = calm or disabled sensor.
catalog.help.SETWINDDIROF: Set wind direction offset (degrees). Saved and applied immediately.
catalog.help.GETWINDDIROF: Read direction offset in degrees (1 decimal place, e.g. -12.3). Used to correct the wind direction reading.
catalog.help.GETWINDDIR: Read calculated wind direction in degrees (0..359.9). From load profile evaluation.
catalog.help.SETTEMPA: Set ambient temperature warning threshold. Value in °C. <=0 disables warning.
catalog.help.SETTEMPM: Set motor temperature warning threshold. Value in °C. <=0 disables warning.
catalog.help.GETTEMPAW: Read ambient temperature warning threshold (°C). 0 disables the warning.
catalog.help.GETTEMPMW: Read motor temperature warning threshold (°C). 0 disables the warning.
catalog.help.GETSWAPTMP: Read temperature sensor assignment. 0 = normal, 1 = swapped.
catalog.help.SETSWAPTEMP: Swaps the motor and ambient temperature readings. Depending on how the sensors are detected, the temperatures may need to be swapped. 0 = normal, 1 = swapped. Saved.
catalog.help.SETCOLDT: Set cold threshold (°C). Below this: extra friction allowed.
catalog.help.GETCOLDT: Read cold threshold (°C). Below this: extra friction allowed.
catalog.help.SETCOLDP: Set extra friction at cold (%). Rec.: 10..25.
catalog.help.GETCOLDP: Read extra friction at cold (%). Allowed addition.
catalog.help.SETCALIGNDG: Calibration: set ramp range (degrees) to ignore. Rec.: 10 (test) or 30 (robust).
catalog.help.GETCALIGNDG: Calibration: read ramp range (degrees) to ignore.
catalog.help.SETSTATMINDG: Statistics: set minimum movement (°). Only movements > value count. Rec.: 30..90.
catalog.help.GETSTATMINDG: Statistics: read minimum movement (°). Only large moves count.
catalog.help.SETDRAG: Set "more friction" threshold (%). Mean for load profile. Rec.: 15..30.
catalog.help.GETDRAG: Read "more friction" threshold (%). Mean value threshold.
catalog.help.SETDRAGBINS: Set proportion of bins above threshold (%). Rec.: 30..70.
catalog.help.GETDRAGBINS: Read proportion of bins above threshold (%). How many angle bins must be above.
catalog.help.SETDRAGPERSIST: Set persistence (number of moves). How many times in a row. Rec.: 3..5.
catalog.help.GETDRAGPERSIST: Read persistence (number of moves). How many large moves in a row needed.
catalog.help.SETWINDPEAK: Wind: set peak threshold (%). For gusts. Rec.: 25..60.
catalog.help.GETWINDPEAK: Wind: read peak threshold (%). For gusts/short high load.
catalog.help.SETWINDCOH: Wind: set minimum coherence (%). How directed the load must be. Rec.: 50..70.
catalog.help.GETWINDCOH: Wind: read minimum coherence (%). How directed the load must be.
catalog.help.SETCAL: Start calibration run (0→360→0), learn current profile. Only when homed (REFF).
catalog.help.CLRSTAT: Clear live statistics. Good before tests, so only current moves count.
catalog.help.GETCALSTATE: Calibration run status. Response: state;progress. state: 0=IDLE,1=RUNNING,2=DONE,3=ABORT.
catalog.help.GETLOADSTAT: Load statistics. Response: mean%;peak%;coh%;moves. Delta from calibration.
catalog.help.GETWIND: Wind estimate from motor load profile. Response: peak%;coh%;dirDeg;peakDeg.
catalog.help.GETCALBINS: Read calibration bins (72 bins). Params: dir;start;count.
catalog.help.GETLIVEBINS: Read live bins (72 bins). Like GETCALBINS, current operating data.
catalog.help.GETACCBINS: Fast current load statistics (72 bins). Params: DIR;START;COUNT. Responds faster than GETLIVEBINS. Works without calibration.
catalog.help.SETACCBINSRST: Reset ACC bin statistics. Only the fast ACC statistics are cleared, not LIVE or calibration.
catalog.help.GETDELTABINS: Read delta bins (live minus cal) in %. Values can be negative.
catalog.help.GETERR: Query current error code. Response ACK_ERR with code or 0.
catalog.help.SETREF: Start homing (parameter 1) or acknowledge a fault on the controller (parameter 0). 0 = acknowledge only at the device, without starting a new homing run. 1 = start homing. Note: While this application shows an error, homing/SETREF often cannot clear the fault reliably. Inspect the rotor and mechanics on site; close the message and restart the application so the display and bus status match again (see also error pop-ups).
catalog.help.SETPOSDG: Start position move in degrees. Target angle e.g. 160.00. For small steps increase ARRTOL if needed.
catalog.help.SETPOSCC: Compass target (display). No position move. Optional angle;rotor bus ID (e.g. 151,30;20) for AZ vs EL.
catalog.help.SETID: Set and save slave address. Note: assign the new address in settings afterwards.
catalog.help.SETDGOFFSET: Set offset (degrees). Subtracted during output/control. Typically 2.25 or 2.50.
catalog.help.GETDGOFFSET: Read offset (degrees). Subtracted during position output.
catalog.help.SETANTOFF1: Set antenna offset 1 (0–360°). Separate per axis (AZ/EL).
catalog.help.SETANTOFF2: Set antenna offset 2 (0–360°). Separate per axis (AZ/EL).
catalog.help.SETANTOFF3: Set antenna offset 3 (0–360°). Separate per axis (AZ/EL).
catalog.help.SETANGLE1: Set antenna beam width 1 (0–360°). Saved in rotor.
catalog.help.SETANGLE2: Set antenna beam width 2 (0–360°). Saved in rotor.
catalog.help.SETANGLE3: Set antenna beam width 3 (0–360°). Saved in rotor.
catalog.help.SETHOMEPWM: Homing: set max PWM (%). Fast homing PWM. Rec.: 60..100.
catalog.help.GETHOMEPWM: Homing: read max PWM (%). Speed during return movement.
catalog.help.SETHOMEBACKOFF: Homing: backoff from limit switch in degrees. Rec.: 10..60.
catalog.help.GETHOMEBACKOFF: Homing: read backoff from limit switch in degrees.
catalog.help.SETHOMEBLSCALE: Homing: backlash calculation scale factor (0–1). Default 0.5. If reversing removes too much travel: increase.
catalog.help.SETHOMERETURN: Set return to 0 after homing. 1=yes, 0=no. Usually 1.
catalog.help.SETHOMETIMEOUT: Homing: set timeout (ms). If homing aborts: increase. Rec.: 60000..120000.
catalog.help.SETPOSTIMEOUT: Set position timeout (ms). Max. time for position move. Rec.: 30000..90000.
catalog.help.GETPOSTIMEOUT: Read position timeout (ms). Max. time for a position move.
catalog.help.SETHANDSPEED: Set hand speed (%). Safe slow movement. Rec.: 10..40.
catalog.help.GETHANDSPEED: Read hand speed (%). Safe slow movement (local/service).
catalog.help.SETDEADMAN: Set deadman/keepalive timeout (ms). 0=off. Rec.: 2000..5000 if master sends regularly.
catalog.help.SETIWARN: Set current warning threshold (mV). Warning only, no stop. Rec.: 200..350.
catalog.help.GETIWARN: Read current warning threshold (mV). Warning only, no stop.
catalog.help.SETIMAX: Set current shutdown threshold (mV). Leads to stop + error. Rec.: 300..600.
catalog.help.GETIMAX: Read current shutdown threshold (mV). Leads to stop + error.
catalog.help.SETARRTOL: Set arrival tolerance (degrees). How close to target. Rec.: 0.02..0.20.
catalog.help.GETARRTOL: Read arrival tolerance (degrees). How close to target.
catalog.help.SETRAMP: Set ramp length (degrees). Accelerate/decelerate over this angle. Rec.: 10..40.
catalog.help.GETRAMP: Read ramp length (degrees). Accelerate/decelerate over this angle.
catalog.help.SETMINPWM: Set min PWM (%). Lower limit for movement. Rec.: 20..35. If stalling: increase MINPWM.
catalog.help.GETMINPWM: Read min PWM (%). Lower limit for movement.
catalog.help.SETSTALLTIMEOUT: Set stall timeout (ms). Time until SE_STALL. Rec.: 1500..4000. If ERR16: increase.
catalog.help.GETSTALLTIMEOUT: Read stall timeout (ms). Time until SE_STALL triggers.
catalog.help.SETMAXPWM: Set max PWM (%) permanently. Global limit for position move. Rec.: 60..100.
catalog.help.GETMAXPWM: Read max PWM (%). Global limit for position move.
catalog.help.SETPWM: Set PWM limit (%) at runtime. Without saving.
catalog.help.GETPWM: Read currently enabled PWM limit (%). Can be influenced by SETMAXPWM/SETPWM.
catalog.help.RESET: Reset everything to factory defaults and restart. All settings will be lost.
catalog.help.SETMINSTALLPWM: Set minimum PWM for stall detection. Prevents stall at very low PWM.
catalog.help.GETMINSTALLPWM: Read minimum PWM for stall detection. Stall is checked from this PWM.
catalog.help.SETSTALLMINCOUNTS: Set minimum encoder counts in stall time window.
catalog.help.GETSTALLMINCOUNTS: Read minimum encoder counts in stall time window.
catalog.help.SETISFILTERLEN: Set current averaging filter length. Larger = smoother, slower. Rec.: 1..32.
catalog.help.GETFILTERLEN: Read current averaging filter length. Belongs to SETISFILTERLEN.
catalog.help.SETISHOLDMS: Set current hard limit hold time (ms). Current limit must be exceeded for this long.
catalog.help.GETISHOLDMS: Read current hard limit hold time (ms).
catalog.help.SETISGRACEMS: Set current protection grace time (ms). Grace period after start/reverse: hard stop ignored.
catalog.help.SETHOMESEEKPPWM: Set additional search PWM for homing search phase. Used when searching for the first limit switch.
catalog.help.GETHOMESEEKPPWM: Read additional search PWM for homing search phase. Used when searching for the first limit switch.
catalog.help.SETENCCRI: Set counts per 360° ring encoder. Typically approx. 160000. Saved.
catalog.help.GETENCCRI: Read counts per 360° ring encoder. Integer for scaling.
catalog.help.SETENCCAX: Set counts per 360° axis/motor encoder. Typically approx. 50000. Saved.
catalog.help.GETENCCAX: Read counts per 360° axis/motor encoder. Integer for scaling.
catalog.help.SETENCTYPE: Set encoder variant. 1 = Axis/Motor, 2 = Ring. Check counts/homing after change.
com0com.description_text:
Some programs – for example Ham Radio Deluxe – can only talk to real serial ports when controlling a radio or rotor.

Here you can create virtual serial ports for the rotor and assign COM ports to your configured rigs. Windows presents them like real ports, so external programs can use them as if the radio or rotor were connected directly to that port.

For the rotor, select „AlfaSpid BIG-RAS AZ/EL" in your external software. For the radios, pick in the external software the same radio that is configured in the Rig-Bridge.

„External" in the assignment refers to the port you have to enter in the external program – it is the other side of the com0com pair.
com0com.chk_verbose_cat_tip:
Detailed diagnostic log for the serial CAT bridge:
logs every incoming byte chunk from the external program, each responder reply
with the returned frequency, every state-cache frequency change (before/after
and source) and the reasons why READFREQs are skipped or discarded.
Only enable for troubleshooting — the log grows very quickly.
Not persisted; switched off again on restart.
com0com.dlg_remove_listener_note: The following PST-serial listener(s) will also be removed: {ports}
com0com.dlg_not_installed_text: setupc.exe was not found. Please install com0com (https://com0com.sourceforge.net/).
com0com.dlg_uac_hint: This action needs administrator rights. Windows will prompt for approval (UAC).
com0com.tooltip_runas: Action runs via UAC (setupc.exe needs admin rights).
pst_serial.hint: One listener serves AZ and EL at the same time (ROT2PROG frame = 13 bytes). For each com0com pair the dropdown lists the port RotorTcpBridge opens. The parenthesised "(external: COMxx)" is the port you have to configure in the external program (e.g. PstRotator) — the other side of the pair.
